item.title: About
item.description:
I am Abhinav, interested in networking, p2p systems and breaking stuff. Welcome to my corner of the The Internet.

You might enjoy browsing through a few of my dev blogs, or simply scroll through my resume to see what I am up to lately.

You can best reach me via e-mail: mail@abhinavagarwalla6@gmail.com.

item.title: Introduced Prometheus metrics to libp2p(-py)
item.description:
Introduced Prometheus/Grafana metrics to libp2p(-py), improving network observability for the core services like Gossipsub, Kad-DHT and connection lifecycle.

PR: https://github.com/libp2p/py-libp2p/pull/1199

item.title: Kedarkantha Peak
item.description:
Went first time trekking, to Kedarkantha peak. The elevation was around ~3800m, spent 3 days in the forest and river rafting in Rishikesh.

Felt good to get away from code, from time to time.

item.title: Introduced AutoTLS in libp2p(-py)
item.description:
Extended the TLS security module in libp2p(-py) to fetch CA authorized TLS certificates using the libp2p Auto-TLS client specification, rather than using self-signed ones.

This will enable protocol execution via web-transport and browsers for libp2p(-py).

PR: https://github.com/libp2p/py-libp2p/pull/1072

item.title: Implemented Floodsub from scratch
item.description:
Wrote a Floodsub module for scratch in my experimental p2p stack.

Learned a great deal how pubsub broadcasting systems behave under the hood with complex rust async designs. Getting ready to write Gossipsub now.

item.title: Rust async pattern -- single owner IO
item.description: Ran into an issue while building my experimental p2p stack in Rust – multiple parts of the code wanted &mut TcpStream. Turned out the design was improper. What worked for me was creating a single IO task owning the socket, with everything else communication via mpsc. Cleaner ownership, fewer borrow issues, and easier to reason about.

item.title: Got a Raspberry Pi 5
item.description:
Bought a Raspberry PI 5.

Planning to do some networking experiments and small infrstructure setups.

item.title: Started writing a p2p stack in Rust
item.description: Started writing an experimental p2p stack, rnet inspired from libp2p specs, to dig deeper into rust and core p2p mechanics.

item.title: Hackathon win @ETHNewDelhi'25
item.description: Attended an IRL hackathon in New Delhi. Built a p2p federated learning platform using libp2p(-py), Akave-O3 and Hedera hashgraph. Won the Filecoin/Akave track at ETHNewDelhi'25.

item.title: Contributions in libp2p(-py) in PLDG Cohort-3
item.description:
Hey folks 👋,
Over the past few months, I’ve been diving deep into the internals of py-libp2p - the python implementation of the libp2p networking stack. I joined in this project on PLDG cohort-3 mostly out of curiosity: what does it really take to make peer-to-peer systems talk to each other? Turns out… a fair bit.
This blog is a casual walkthrough of what I’ve have been working on — things like getting Python node to talk with Rust peers, cleaning up old TODOs in the codebase, and helping refactor core modules like the PeerStore. If you’re curious about open source, P2P protocols, or python networking internals, this should be an interesting read.
I’ll skip the heavy theory (for the most part) and just talk about the work, the weird bugs, what I learned, and where it’s all going. Let’s get into it.

item.title: Selected for Protocol Labs Dev Guild
item.description:
Got selected for Protocol Labs Dev Guild.

Excited to go deeper into p2p infrascture and libp2p ecosystem.

item.title: Learning document on iptables
item.description: Found a great document to learn about firewall management and iptables in deep.

item.title: Built a VPN gateway with Wireguard
item.description:
Built a VPN gateway using Wireguard, Docker and a nginx server, restricting the server access to only allow wireguard-client IP addresses by modifying the firefall rules for the docker network.

It was fun, wrote a blog about this.

item.title: Building a Covert LAN - A Dockerized VPN Gateway using WireGuard and Nginx
item.description:
Establishing a secure, private network by integrating Wireguard VPN with Docker container services to ensure exclusive access through encrypted connections.

Introduction

In an increasing noisy internet, privacy isn’t just about encrypting your data - it’s also about controlling who gets to see and expose your services in the first place. Imagining running a set of internal services, say a dashboard, an internal wiki, or a media server. You don’t want these exposed to the internet, and even your local network feels too open. What if you could make them disappear entirely from everything except a private VPN?

item.title: Automated Firewall management usign Docker and Cron
item.description:
Simulating automated firewall configuration using Docker, cron jobs, and UFW to ensure consistent security policies across multiple server roles

Introduction

Managing firewall rules across multiple servers can be tedious, especially in dynamic environments where configurations change frequently. In this blog we will explore how to automate firewall management using Docker, cron jobs and UFW (Uncomplicated Firewall). We’ll simulate a real-world scenario where different server roles require unique server rules and ensure they are consistently enforced using a centralized repository.

item.title: Build on AI-agents for the first time
item.description: First working with AI agents, built a loyalty platform on NEAR blockchain by using Near AI-agent kit for agent driven user interaction, and smart contract execution. Had fun.

item.title: Weekend hackathon win
item.description: Participated in a hackathon over the weekend. Built a cool event hosting platform on Vara blockchain. Won a 1st prize in the beginner track. Getting more comfortable working under tight constraints.

item.title: Pool prize at @ETHIndia'24 hackathon
item.description: Attended an IRL hackthon in Bangalore. Won a pool prize building on Lit Protocol’s decetralized encryption/decryption API.

item.title: Implementing Blind Computation using Nillion Protocol
item.description:
A comprehensive guide on how to implement blind computations (computing on encrypted variables without ever decrypting them) using Nillion protocol.

Introduction

So what is the meaning of blind computation ? What are its advantages ?

I recently participated in ETHGlobal’s ETHOnline’24 hackathon, and my team built a decentarlized roulette platform (FairBet) with the help of Nillion and Sign Protocol, and won a partner prize too !!

%[https://x.com/lla_dane/status/1839514686351421874]

I worked on implementing a blind computation infrastructure using Nillion so that the result of the game gets calculated not on the open backend or frontend which could be easily manipulated by malicious entities, but on a secure blockchain environment on which the variables are dispersed over the network and also encrypted when they are computed upon !! So not chance of tampering with the result.

item.title: Wrote on Nillion's Blind Computation API
item.description: Wrote a blog on my contribution to our project in @EthOnline'24 hackthon, on Nillion’s blind computation API.

item.title: Hackathon win @EthSingapore'24
item.description: First time travel to Singapore, amazing country. Won a prize building on Oasis ROFL API with team.

item.title: Hackathon win @EthOnline'24
item.description: Participated in my first hackathon. Won a prize in Nillion track building on its blind compuation API.

item.title: Completed Summer of Bitcoin '24
item.description:
Got selected for Summer of Bitcoin 2024, contributing to Floresta, a lightweight bitcoin client.

Learned a lot about navigating large codebases and p2p mechanics used in decentralized systems.

item.title: Improve Floresta's testing - Part 2
item.description:
This blog covers the progress of my Summer of Bitcoin porject - Improve Floresta’s testing

Floresta ?

Floresta a lightweight Bitcoin full node implementation written in Rust. powered by Utreexo a novel dynamic accumulator designed for the Bitcoin UTXO set, with an integrated Electrum server.

My progress :

All my related PRs after mid-evaluation:

  • https://github.com/vinteumorg/Floresta/pull/200

  • https://github.com/vinteumorg/Floresta/pull/202

  • https://github.com/vinteumorg/Floresta/pull/203

  • https://github.com/vinteumorg/Floresta/pull/214

Floresta-wire :

As I discussed before in my previous blog about my progress, I discussed about 3 node instances, that make up Floresta’s node infrastructure:

item.title: Improve Floresta's testing - Part 1
item.description:
This blog covers the progress of my Summer of Bitcoin project till mid-evaluation:
Improve the testing environment of Floresta.

Floresta ?

Floresta a lightweight Bitcoin full node implementation written in Rust. powered by Utreexo a novel dynamic accumulator designed for the Bitcoin UTXO set, with an integrated Electrum server.

At the beginning Floresta only had some basic functional and unit testing, far from ideal. My job is to build a robust testing infrastructure, improving the quality and assurance of the software.

item.title: First open source fellowship
item.description:
Got selected for Summer of Bitcoin 2024. First real step into working on open source projects in a structured way.

Wrote a blog about the selection process too.

item.title: Getting selected in Summer of Bitcoin'24
item.description:
What is SoB ?

Summer of Bitcoin is a global program focused on bringing more student developers into the open source software development in the Bitcoin community. Students work with an open-source organisation on a 12 week programming project during their summer break from school.

This blog highlights journey of getting selected into the program from the very beginning. I believe their is no defined path for getting selected in such open-source fellowships. Everyone has a unique journey, and you can take a few things from other journeys to write yours :) because the essence of each one is similar.

item.title: Build Logs
item.description:
Github | Linkedln | Twitter

Open Source:

  • Libp2p(-py): The Python implementation of the libp2p networking stack.

    Pull Requests:

    • Prometheus metrics for libp2p protocols: #1199
    • Add TLS in the security options: #1085
    • Auto-TLS support for py-libp2p: #1072
    • Improve Floodsub in PubSub module: #1064
    • Add pnet support with PSK-based connection wrapping: #1002
    • libp2p-record module in reference with go-libp2p-record: #890
    • Signed-Peer-Record support in Pubsub/Gossipsub message transfer: #889
    • Signed-Peer-Record support in KAD-DHT message transfer mechanism: #815
    • TODO: throttle on async validators: #755
    • Introduce Certified-Address-Book interface for Peer-Store: #753
    • Added tests for identify push concurrency cap under high peer load: #708
    • fix added negotiate timeout to MuxerMultistream: #696
    • fix removed dummy ID(b) from upgrade_security for inbound connections: #681
    • Updated examples to automatically use random port: #661
    • Matching py-libp2p <-> go-libp2p PeerStore Implementation: #648
    • TODO: Handle ls command in multiselect.py: #622
    • TODO: Parse listen_addrs to set transport in build_swarm: #616
  • py-multiaddr: Multiaddr implementation for libp2p(-py)

    Pull Requests:

item.title: Resume
item.description:
Experience

Contributor to Libp2p(-py) networking stack

May 2025 - now

  • 22 pull requests to github.com/libp2p/py-libp2p and github.com/multiformats/py-multiaddr.
  • Alligned PeerStore and record handling with go-libp2p specifications, improving protocol correctness and interoperability.
  • Introduce peer autenticity mechanisms, including Certified-Address-Book and signed-peer-records, integrated across PubSub and Kademlia DHT message propagation.
  • Expanded multiaddr protocol support in WebRTC, Noise, garlic, ipcidr, http-path, improving compatibility with mordern libp2p transports.
  • Extended security and transport layers with Auto-TLS support, and PSK-based private networking (pnet). See #1072 and #1002.
  • Introduce Prometheus metrics to the core services, improving network oberservability.

Summer of Bitcoin Fellow - Floresta (Bitcoin Client)

May 2024 - August 2024
